description_long: This kiosk features a rectangular counter setup with a white base and a backdrop of three weathered, industrial-style panels. The front counter displays trays of food items, possibly baked goods or snacks, within a glass or acrylic enclosure. To the right, there are two large transparent tanks, likely for live seafood or aquatic displays, filled with water and substrate. Behind the counter, the wall is adorned with two printed posters, one advertising 'SOY BEAN' and another with a group of people, suggesting a market or educational theme. The flooring includes a green mat in front of the tanks, providing a non-slip surface for staff or customers. The overall design is functional and utilitarian, suitable for a market, fair, or educational exhibit.
description: A rectangular kiosk with a white counter, food display, two large water tanks, and weathered industrial wall panels with posters.
description_view_0: Front view showing the food display case, two large water tanks, and posters on the weathered wall panels.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the kiosk, the green mat, and the alignment of the tanks and counter.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the full expanse of the weathered wall panels with no visible openings or features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the arrangement of the counter, tanks, and the green mat in front of the tanks.
attribute_signage_type: printed signs on the rear wall panels; one reads 'SOY BEAN', another features a group of people; both are poster-style and affixed to the wall.
attribute_roof_design: flat, no visible overhangs or decorative elements, constructed from the same material as the wall panels.
attribute_architectural_style: industrial utilitarian with market or educational exhibit influences
